behavior_summary: Provides NPC behavior and dialogue for Bentic (a librarian). The script supplies: a short 'U8P_BENTIC::look' description that uses learned-name/job flags, a full conversational 'U8P_BENTIC::use' flow with dynamic menus and flag updates, time-of-day scheduling, quick-area (enter/leave) reactions that start brief interactions, theft handling for watched item types, and a reaction-to-attack handler that can summon guards and emit alarm barks.

Main player-facing interaction is U8P_BENTIC::use — it begins a conversation (METHOD::beginConversationNoMap), builds/modifies a menu (local4) based on flags (benticMet, benticName, benticJob, benticStoryTold, haveAvaName), captures the player's menu choice (local1), updates flags (sets benticMet, benticName, benticJob) and emits multiple DialogueLine texts.

U8P_BENTIC::look emits a short descriptive bark when the player examines the NPC. It first checks Npc::isDead(this) and otherwise picks from 'Bentic the Librarian', 'Bentic', 'librarian', or 'man' depending on benticName and benticJob flags.
Scheduling (U8P_BENTIC::schedule) uses FREE::getTimeOfDayPeriod() to set temporary locals encoding U8P_BENTIC::schedule/timing; fallback calls METHOD::func133F.

Proximity triggers: U8P_BENTIC::enterFastArea clears a reference (FREE::resetRef(0x0205, this)) and runs U8P_BENTIC::func3A25 to spawn a short timed interaction; U8P_BENTIC::leaveFastArea clears the ref and, if dead, teleports the NPC randomly (Npc::teleport).

Theft handling (U8P_BENTIC::AvatarStoleSomething(uword)) calls METHOD::func15E7 then checks Item::getType(item). For specific item type IDs (0x0265 or 0x0120) it clears refs 0x0200 and 0x0205, barks 'Please return that within two weeks!', and calls U8P_BENTIC::func0216 for follow-up.

Combat reaction (U8P_BENTIC::cast(uword)) inspects Npc::getTarget(this); if the target is the Avatar (inferred when target == 1) it calls CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime and emits one of two alarm barks chosen probabilistically; when no target it uses a small retry/timer counter and spawns a short timeout (FREE::func1C19).

Small utility routines: U8P_BENTIC::func3A25 orchestrates delayed spawns and then calls U8P_BENTIC::func3AA1; U8P_BENTIC::func3AA1 selects randomized short barks about books/interest and sometimes calls METHOD::func15E7.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BENTIC::look: if Npc::isDead(this) -> bark 'Bentic the dead librarian' and suspend; else branch on flags benticName and benticJob to select one of four short barks, then suspend.

U8P_BENTIC::use: initialize mentionedPagan=0, call METHOD::beginConversationNoMap, build local4 menu array, loop presenting menu (Ask/SuspendAssign -> local1), compare local1 with option strings (strcmp) and run branch actions (dialogue lines, set flags benticMet/benticName/benticJob, add/remove menu options) until 'bye' is chosen (ConversationLoop/end).
U8P_BENTIC::schedule: if Npc::isDead(this) then return; otherwise set scheduling locals local3=0x29 and choose local2/local1/local0 based on FREE::getTimeOfDayPeriod(); fallback -> METHOD::func133F.
U8P_BENTIC::enterFastArea -> if not dead: FREE::resetRef(0x0205,this) -> U8P_BENTIC::func3A25 (short timed interaction).
U8P_BENTIC::leaveFastArea: FREE::resetRef(0x0205,this) always; if Npc::isDead(this) then Npc::teleport(0,0,urandom(1000),urandom(1000),this).

U8P_BENTIC::AvatarStoleSomething(item): METHOD::func15E7(); if Item::getType(&item) == 0x0265 or 0x0120 then FREE::resetRef(0x0200,this) & FREE::resetRef(0x0205,this), bark 'Please return that within two weeks!', call U8P_BENTIC::func0216.

U8P_BENTIC::cast(selector): uses local1 counter to spawn FREE::func1C19 (timer) when no target; if Npc::getTarget(this) == 1 then call CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime and randomly choose between two alarm barks.
open_questions: What is the concrete purpose of METHOD::func15E7? It is called in theft and utility flows and likely records/alerts something, but its exact effect is unknown. (confidence: medium)
What does METHOD::func133F do as the U8P_BENTIC::schedule fallback? It likely performs a default movement/U8P_BENTIC::schedule action but the engine semantics are not visible here. (confidence: low)

What is implemented inside U8P_BENTIC::func0216 (referred by U8P_BENTIC::AvatarStoleSomething) ? It is invoked for stronger theft handling but its behavior is not present in this excerpt. (confidence: low)

Semantics of reference slots 0x0205 and 0x0200: they are cleared in several places (enter/leave fast area, theft) and appear to be interaction/tracking refs — exact mapping to engine reference slots is inferred but not certain. (confidence: medium)
Whether Npc::getTarget(...) == 1 definitively means the Avatar: this is inferred from context (guard call and alarm lines) but not explicitly confirmed. (confidence: medium)

main_function.name: U8P_BENTIC::look
main_function.purpose: Provide a short descriptive/bark response when the player examines the NPC. Uses death state and learned-name/job flags to choose wording.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the game when the player examines/looks at the NPC (U8P_BENTIC::look interaction).
Branches on Npc::isDead(this) and flags benticName and benticJob.

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::look
function_map.role: Player 'U8P_BENTIC::look' description handler; emits short bark based on dead/alive and learned flags.

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::use
function_map.role: Conversation entry and loop; builds dynamic menus, captures choices, emits dialogue, and sets persistent flags (benticMet, benticName, benticJob).

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic U8P_BENTIC::schedule/task setter using FREE::getTimeOfDayPeriod to pick time-specific locals; controls routine behavior while alive.

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Triggered by actor entering a fast-interaction radius; clears ref 0x0205 and starts a short interaction via U8P_BENTIC::func3A25.

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Triggered when actor leaves fast area; clears ref 0x0205 and if NPC is dead teleports corpse randomly for cleanup.

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: Handles theft events (param: item handle); logs/calls METHOD::func15E7, checks item type, clears refs and issues a polite return request for watched items, then calls U8P_BENTIC::func0216.

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::cast
function_map.role: Reaction-to-attack/U8P_BENTIC::cast handler; may spawn timeouts, notifies guard system on player attack, and barks alarm lines.

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::func3A25
function_map.role: Utility timer/orchestrator called from U8P_BENTIC::enterFastArea; schedules delayed spawns and then calls U8P_BENTIC::func3AA1 and an external notifier.

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::func3AA1
function_map.role: Randomized short-bark utility (book/interest lines) used by proximity/utility flows; sometimes calls METHOD::func15E7 and may spawn a timer.

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Engine query to detect NPC death; used to short-circuit behaviors and alter 'U8P_BENTIC::look' output.

function_map.name: METHOD::beginConversationNoMap
function_map.role: Engine call to start conversation UI without changing map; used at start of U8P_BENTIC::use.

function_map.name: FREE::getTimeOfDayPeriod
function_map.role: Engine query used by U8P_BENTIC::schedule to choose time-of-day dependent U8P_BENTIC::schedule values.

internal_calls.purpose: Start the short timed/ambient interaction sequence when an actor enters the fast area.
Invoke the randomized short-bark routine after scheduled waits/spawns.
Call local follow-up theft handler when a watched item was stolen.

external_calls.purpose: Open the conversation UI and begin dialogue without changing map state.
Obtain current time-of-day period to pick U8P_BENTIC::schedule/timing locals.
Fallback U8P_BENTIC::schedule action when time-of-day branches do not match; likely default movement or routine invocation.
Clear reference slot 0x0205 to avoid lingering interaction state before starting proximity routine.
Clear reference slot 0x0205 on fast-area exit for cleanup.
Teleport the NPC to a random coordinate when dead; used for corpse repositioning/cleanup.
Notify or log a theft/crime-related event before further handling.
Check the stolen item's type id to decide whether to escalate reaction.
Clear reference slots 0x0200 and 0x0205 as part of theft reaction cleanup.
Determine the NPC's current combat/attack target to decide whether to call guards and bark.
Notify the guard/crime system when the Avatar is attacking the NPC.
Spawn a short timed process used as a retry/timeout when no target is present.
Spawn timed callbacks used to U8P_BENTIC::schedule the sequence of delayed barks/behavior.
Notify or record the fact that a short bark or ambient event was emitted (in several random branches).

reads.name: benticMet
reads.likely_effect: Used to select greeting/menu variants (whether player has met Bentic before).

reads.name: haveAvaName
reads.likely_effect: Decides whether the conversation uses the player's name in greeting lines.

reads.name: benticStoryTold
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether a story-related menu option ('Orlok says you have a treasure map') is included.

reads.name: benticName
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine if the NPC's personal name should be used in descriptions and dialogue options.

reads.name: benticJob
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine if the NPC's job/title should be used and to unlock job-related dialogue.

reads.name: benticGivenBook
reads.likely_effect: Checked to avoid offering 'What are you reading?' repeatedly (prevents re-offer).

writes.name: mentionedPagan
writes.likely_effect: Initialized to 0 at conversation start to reset this conversational flag.

writes.name: benticMet
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when player asks 'What is your name?'; marks the NPC as met for future dialogs.

writes.name: benticName
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the player asks for the NPC's name; changes future U8P_BENTIC::look/dialogue wording to U8P_BENTIC::use 'Bentic'.

writes.name: benticJob
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the player asks 'What do you do?'; unlocks job-related story/dialogue and impacts 'U8P_BENTIC::look'.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides NPC behavior and dialogue for Bentic (a librarian). The script supplies: a short 'U8P_BENTIC::look' description that uses learned-name/job flags, a full conversational 'U8P_BENTIC::use' flow with dynamic menus and flag updates, time-of-day scheduling, quick-area (enter/leave) reactions that start brief interactions, theft handling for watched item types, and a reaction-to-attack handler that can summon guards and emit alarm barks.
